original: Pope Gregory IV decrees All Saints Day a holy day of obligation for the Catholic religion
First recorded use of modern name for Austria in the 'Ostarrîchi Document'
Phillip II crowned King of France at age 14 in Reims, with his father Louis VII in ill health
Earl William II of Holland crowned as King of Germany
Black Death Massacre: Duke of Brabant orders execution of all Jews in Brussels, accusing them of poisoning the wells during the Bubonic Plague; part of a wave of pogroms across Western Europe
Albert VI of Habsburg lays siege to his brother Holy Roman  Emperor Frederick III occupying Vienna
Portuguese hold a mass to celebrate the completion of Fort Manuel their first fort and the first European fort in India, at Cochin (Kochi)
All Saints Flood, tidal wave in the North Sea devastates the coast from Holland to Jutland; killing more than 1,000 people.
Army of Moroccan ruler Ahmad al-Mansur led by Spanish Eunuch Jawdar leaves Marrakesh to cross the Sahara to conquer the Songhai Empire
Shakespeare's romantic comedy play "The Tempest" is performed at court in front of James I at Whitehall Palace - its first recorded performance
(22 October O.S.) Time of Troubles in Russia: Moscow, Kitai-gorod, is captured by Russian troops under command of Dmitry Pozharsky
Fire at Plymouth, Massachusetts, destroys several buildings
The English crown colony of New York is subdivided into 12 counties.
Lisbon earthquake kills more than 50,000 in Portugal
Stamp Act goes into effect in British colonies
Mission San Juan Capistrano founded in California
Maryland grants citizenship to Lafayette and his descendants
First free school in NYC (African Free School) opens
Delegates meet at Chillicothe, Ohio, to form a state constitutional convention.
First published reference to poker (as Mississippi riverboat game)
Classes begin at Boston Female Medical College, 1st US female medical college
Dutch government of Thorbecke forms
The current Cape Lookout, North Carolina lighthouse is lit for the first time. Its first-order Fresnel lens can be seen for 19 miles (30 kilometers)
-8] Averell's Raid (on)to Lewisburg, West Virginia
Fortifications built on Angel Island (San Francisco Bay) by troops
Zutphen-Fishing Dutch railway opens
Deli Me forms T B V tobacco in Sumatra
US Weather Bureau begins operations (24 locations)
King William III of the Netherlands opens the North Sea Canal (Amsterdam-IJmuiden)
New Zealand's provincial government system is dissolved.
Dutch government of Heemskerk-Van Lynden resigns
Edward Scripps & John Sweeney found Penny Press (Cleveland Press)
Gaelic Athletic Association is established in a meeting at Hayes' Hotel, Thurles, County Tipperary; Clare teacher Michael Cusack is credited as founder; objective to promote Irish sport & games
Ananda College, a leading Buddhist school in Sri Lanka is established with 37 students
First bare-breasted women (Zulu) to appear in National Geographic Magazine
Italian football club Juventus F.C. is founded by students in Turin as Sport-Club Juventus
Sigma Phi Epsilon, the largest national male collegiate fraternity is established at Richmond College, in Richmond, VA.
France and Italy sign an Entente under which Italy agrees to remain neutral if France is attacked; this is France's attempt to neutralize the Triple Entente
Notre Dame upsets Army, 35-13 in the colleges' inaugural football game; quarterback Gus Dorais (14 of 17 passes, 243 yards, 2 TDs) and receiver Knute Rockne use forward pass effectively
German-British naval battle at Coronel, Chile
Pope Benedictus Xv's encyclical Ad beatissimi, against integrity
Parris Island is officially designated a US Marine Corps Recruit Depot.
Broadway theatre owner and producer Harry H. Frazee and Hugh Ward buy MLB club the Boston Red Sox for approximately $700,000 from Joseph Lannin
Paul Miliukov delivers in the Russian State Duma the famous "stupidity or treason" speech, precipitating the downfall of the Boris Stürmer government
102 die in a NYC BMT subway derailment at Malbone Street Brooklyn
Yugoslav battleship Viribus Unitis sunk by Italians
American fishing schooner 'Esperanto' defeats Canadian yacht 'Delawana' in the first International Fishing Schooner Championship Races off Halifax, Nova Scotia
National Birth Control League & Voluntary Parenthood League merge as American Birth Control League
In an all-American bout, Mickey Walker upsets defending champion Jack Britton in a 15-round points decision at Madison Square Garden, NYC; wins world welterweight boxing crown
Bruno E. Lucander forms Aero E/Y (Finnair)
Boston Bruins officially join the NHL, becoming the first United States based team to enter the League; Montreal Maroons also join NHL, but only last until 1938
VARA, Vereniging van Workers Radio Amateurs forms in Amsterdam
US Air Commerce Act passes
1st celebration of Authors' Day
Turkey passes a new alphabet law switching from the Arabic to the Latin alphabet
Lundy Island, part of British Isles, issue its own stamps
Dupont introduces synthetic rubber
Professional Rodeo Cowboys Association (then Cowboys' Turtle Association) is established after disagreement with rodeo promoter W.T. Johnson, who finally gives in to cowboys' pay demands
German colonel-general Gerd von Runstedt retires

Seabiscuit, with George Woolf aboard, beats 1937 Triple Crown winner War Admiral by 3 lengths in Pimlico track record time to win $15,000 in what is regarded as one of the greatest match races in horse racing history
First animal conceived by artificial insemination (rabbit) displayed
First jet plane, Heinkel He 178, demonstrated to German Air Ministry
1st US air raid shelter, Fleetwood, Pa
Dutch "Curfew" forms (12 AM - 4 AM)
10th day of battle at El Alamein
John H. Johnson publishes 1st issue of Negro Digest
Dim-out ban lifted in San Francisco Bay area
US troops land on Bougainville Island on Solomon Island
World War II: Units of the British Army land at Walcheren in the Netherlands
Zeeuws & Flanders liberated
First issue of Ebony magazine published by John H. Johnson
Charles S Johnson becomes 1st black president of Fisk University in Nashville, Tennessee
First Basketball Association of America game; New York Knicks beat Toronto Huskies, 68-66 at Maple Leaf Gardens in Toronto
Future pope Karol Wojtyla of Poland is ordained to Catholic priesthood
West German state of Niedersachsen (Lower Saxony) created
First Aloha Week Parade held in Hawaii
UN trusteeship for Nauru granted to Australia, NZ & UK
Mao's Red army conquers Mukden, Manchuria
82°F highest temperature ever recorded in Cleveland in Nov
1st H Bomb test, code named "Ivy Mike", detonates on Enewetak Atoll in the Marshall Islands, the north Pacific Ocean
Jet magazine founded by John H. Johnson
US performs 1st atomic explosion witnessed by troops, Nevada Test Site, New Mexico
Czech long distance runner Emile Zatopek sets world 10,000m record 29:01.6 & 6 mile mark 28:08.4 in Stara Boleslav, Czech Republic
KCEN TV channel 6 in Temple-Waco, TX (NBC/ABC) begins broadcasting
KMGH TV channel 7 in Denver, CO (CBS) begins broadcasting
WHEC TV channel 10 in Rochester, NY (CBS) begins broadcasting
India takes over administration of 4 French Indian settlements
KUON TV channel 12 in Lincoln, NB (PBS) begins broadcasting
The Front de Libération Nationale fires the first shots of the Algerian War of Independence against France.
Time bomb aboard United DC-6 kills 44 above Longmont, Colorado
Delhi becomes a union territory of India
Indian state of Madhya Pradesh is formed
Indian states of Punjab, Patiala and most of the East Punjab States Union merge as the Punjab State
Karnataka (then known as Mysore State) is formed from the merge of Kannada-speaking regions in India
Kerala state is formed in India
Nagy government of Hungary withdraws from Warsaw Pact
PopePius XIIpublishes encyclical Laetamur admodum
KVII TV channel 7 in Amarillo, Texas (ABC) begins broadcasting
KXGN TV channel 5 in Glendive, Montana (CBS/NBC/ABC) begins broadcasting
Mackinac Straits Bridge, Michigan, world longest suspension bridge between anchorages opens
WICZ TV channel 40 in Binghamton, New York (NBC) begins broadcasting
USSR performs nuclear test
WOV-AM in NYC changes call letters to WADO
The Benelux Economic Union Treaty goes in to effect, establishing a free trade area between Belgium, the Netherlands, and Luxembourg
Greece enters European Common Market
KYVE TV channel 47 in Yakima, WA (PBS) begins broadcasting
Noble Prize for Physics awarded to Russian physicist Lev Landau for "theories for condensed matter, especially liquid helium"
US performs atmospheric nuclear test at Johnston Island
USSR launches Mars 1; radio contact lost before arrival at Mars
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
WNYC TV channel 31 in New York, NY (PBS) begins broadcasting
WZZM TV channel 13 in Grand Rapids, MI (ABC) begins broadcasting
Cleveland running backJim Brownruns for 149 yards in Browns' 30-17 win at Pittsburgh to become the first NFL player to exceed 10,000 yards career rushing
Kansas City Chiefs quarterback Len Dawson passes for 435 yards and 6 TDs in 49-39 win over Denver Broncos at Municipal Stadium
Vietcong-assault on airport Bien Hoa at Saigon
Ernie Terrellretains WBA heavyweight boxing title; beats Canadian George Chuvalo in 15 round points decision in Toronto
Promoter Bill Graham books his 1st concert at Fillmore Auditorium, San Francisco, a benefit for political satirists 'The San Francisco Mime Troupe"
Trackless trolley plunged into Nile River drowning 74 (Cairo Egypt)
Indian Haryana state created from Punjab; Chandigarh terr created
NFL awards a franchise to New Orleans; name "Saints" alludes to November 1, All Saints Day in Catholic faith
William Dana in X-15 reaches 93 km
Motion Picture Association of America introduces rating system (G, M, R, X)
University of Suriname opens
The Beatles' "Abbey Road" album goes #1 in US and stays #1 for 11 weeks
Fire at Club Cinq-Sept Discotheque in Saint-Laurent-du-Pont, France, kills 146, as all emergency exits were padlocked
First NFL regular season New York Giants-Jets game; Giants win 22-10 at Shea Stadium
KGTF TV channel 12 in Agana, GU (PBS) begins broadcasting
1st gay themed TV movie - "That Certain Summer"
Germaoin Gagnon scores 1st Islander hat trick
Indian state of Mysore is renamed Karnataka to represent all the regions within Karunadu
Fire kills 189 in less than 25 min (Sao Paulo Brazil)
UN affirms independence of Cyprus
Britain gives Gilbert Island (Kiribati) self rule
W German Generals Krupinski and Franke admit to having been Nazis
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Bolivia military coup under General Busch, President Guevara flees
Tanker Burmah Agate off Galveston Bay, Texas, spills 10.7 m gallons of oil, in US's worst oil spill disaster
US Federal government proposes making a $1.5 billion loan to Chrysler
USSR performs nuclear test
1st Class US Mail raised from 18 cents to 20 cents
Antigua & Barbuda gains independence from Britain (National Day)
Honda becomes the first Asian automobile company to produce cars in the United States with the opening of their factory in Marysville, Ohio. The Honda Accord is the first car produced there.
First NBA game at LA Memorial Sports Arena - LA Clippers beat NY Knicks, 107-105
Larry Shue's "Foreigner" premieres in NYC
Willem de Kooning's "Two Women" sells for $1,980,000, a then record for a contemporary work of art and for a living artist
Netherlands decides definitive sites for cruise missiles
King Johnof England begins imprisoning Jews
Michelangelo's paintings on ceiling of the Sistine Chapel in Vatican City first exhibited to the public after four years of work
William Shakespeare's tragedy "Othello" is first presented
French KingLouis XIIIoccupies La Rochelle
Robert Hooke's landmark work "Micrographia" with drawings using a microscope and coining the word "cell" is published by the Royal Society
French KingLouis XIVand Holy Roman Emperor Leopold I sign secret anti-Dutch treaty
John Adamsbecomes the first US President to live in the White House
Congress of Vienna opens to re-draw the European political map after the defeat of France, in theNapoleonic Wars
GeneralGeorge McClellanmade general in chief of Union armies
PopeLeo XIIIpublishes encyclical Immortale Dei
Nicholas IIbecomes the new Tsar of Russia after the death of his father, TsarAlexander III
Vaccine for diphtheria announced by DrÉmile Rouxof Paris
George Bernard Shaw's comedy play "John Bull's Other Island" premieres in London
First issue of "Crisis" published by editorW.E.B. Du Bois
Less than a week after the US non-intervention promise, PresidentWoodrow Wilsondemands that Mexican dictatorVictoriano Huertaresign
Paul von Hindenburgnamed marshal of the Eastern front
British AdmiralDavid Beattybecomes First Sea Lord
Queen Wilhelminaopens Dutch Historical Maritime museum in Amsterdam
Wernher von Braunnamed head of German liquid-fuel rocket program
T. S. Eliot's play "Murder in the Cathedral" premieres in London
Benito Mussolinidescribes alliance between Italy and Germany as an "axis"
Stalinists execute by shooting Pastor Paul Hamberg and seven members of Azerbaijan's Lutheran community (including three women).
NL batting champion Ernie Lombardi of the Cincinnati Reds is named National League MVP; first catcher to win award; Boston Red Sox 1st basemanJimmie Foxxtakes AL award
PopePius XIIpublishes encyclical Sertum laetitiae
Ansel Adamsshoots 'Moonrise, Hernandez, New Mexico', one of his most famous photographs
Yugoslav nationalist force the Chetniks attacksTito's partisans in Uzice, Yugoslavia
Celtics' forward Chuck Cooper becomes first African American to play in the NBA in Boston's 107-84 loss at Fort Wayne Pistons; future Hall of FamerBob Cousyalso debuts for Celtics

Puerto Rican nationalists Griselio Torresola and Oscar Collazo attempt to assassinate US PresidentHarry Trumanat Blair House in Washington D.C.; attack thwarted by White House Police, including Officer Leslie Coffelt who was killed in the line of duty
Brooklyn Dodgers catcherRoy Campanellawins first of his 3 National League MVP awards
"Ivy Mike", the first thermonuclear weapon to utilize the H-bomb design ofEdward TellerandStanislaw Ulam, is detonated in the Marshall Islands, Pacific Ocean
GeneralFulgencio Batistaelected President of Cuba
US Senate admonishesJoseph McCarthybecause of his slander campaigns
Nobel for physics awarded toWilliam Shockley,Walter H. BrattainandJohn Bardeen
Future Pro Football Hall of Fame running backJim Brownruns for 5 touchdowns in Cleveland Browns, 38-31 win over Baltimore Colts at Memorial Stadium
Montreal Canadiens’ goal tenderJacques Plantebecomes first NHL net-minder to wear a fibreglass protective face mask
Patrice Lumumbaarrested in Belgian Congo arrested for inciting an anti-colonial riot in Stanleyville
Houston Oilers quarterbackGeorge Blandaattempts an AFL record 68 passes (37 completions) in 24-10 loss v Buffalo Bills at War Memorial Stadium
LA Dodgers pitcherSandy Koufaxbecomes first 3-time winner of Cy Young Award; unanimous winner for 2nd straight year; final year only 1 award given for pitchers in both major leagues
"Cool Hand Luke", a prison drama film starringPaul Newman, George Kennedy, andStrother Martin, is released
Apple Records issues its first LP in the UK, "Wonderwall Music",  the film score byGeorge Harrisonis also the first solo album by any of the Beatles
Swedish pop group Festfolk, later known asABBA, play their first ever concert, at a restaurant in Gothenburg, Sweden
Eisenhowerdollar put into circulation
Family Productions releases "Cold Spring Harbor", singer-songwriterBilly Joel's debut studio album; a mastering error caused music to be sped up
US PresidentJimmy Carterraises the minimum wage from $2.30 to $3.35 an hour, effective from 1st Jan 1981
Darrell Waltripwins his 4th straight NASCAR Cup race, the American 500 at North Carolina Motor Speedway; tiesRichard Pettyrecord 1967; 12th win of season; goes on to win Winston Cup crown
Andrew "Dice" Clay and George Wendt appear in "Trick or Treatment"
MLB owners vote not to renew CommissionerBowie Kuhn's contract; AL owners vote for Kuhn, 11-3, and NL, 7-5, 2 shy of 3/4 majority required for re-election; eventually replaced byPeter Ueberroth
The Black Death reaches London on or about this date
WHSmith opens its 1st railway bookstall, at Euston Station in London, England
The first aerial bomb is dropped by an Italian pilot on Turkish troops in Libya during the Italo-Turkish War
Mustafa Kemal Ataturk takes Constantinople from Mehmed VI, proclaiming the Republic of Turkey and bringing an end to the Ottoman Empire
Graf Zeppelinsets airship distance record of 6384 km
Japanese naval staff offiicers Suzuki & Maejima arrive at Pearl Harbor
